commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bd682238a2ec56886368d2a34d5f63b7bb818652)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a remote-base-url guard to Ollama discovery so non-local configured base URLs skip automatic model discovery unless explicit models are configured, plus tests for remote and local cases.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +134. Total +140 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects: CI reproduces the typecheck and lint failures, and source inspection shows the new guard returns before `buildProvider` for any non-local configured Ollama base URL. I did not run a live Ollama Cloud scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer revision for a too-broad discovery guard, failing type/lint checks, and missing real behavior proof; automation should not repair-marker this while the external proof gate is still unmet.

target.title: fix(ollama): skip auto-discovery for remote/cloud base URLs
target.body:
## Summary

- When the Ollama provider base URL points to a remote/cloud instance (e.g. `ollama.com`), the plugin now skips auto-discovery of all available models via `/api/tags`.
- Cloud instances are shared tenants where the provider manages the model catalog; users should only get models they explicitly configure.
- Local/loopback URLs still auto-discover as before.

## Why

Users connecting to cloud Ollama providers (like `ollama.com`) were getting 44+ models auto-discovered and merged into their config on every restart, even when they only wanted one model. This made model pruning impossible — the discovered models kept coming back.

## Changes

- `extensions/ollama/src/discovery-shared.ts`: Added remote-baseUrl guard in `resolveOllamaDiscoveryResult`. When the base URL is remote and no explicit models are configured, return null instead of calling `buildProvider` to auto-discover.
- `extensions/ollama/src/discovery-shared.test.ts`: Added 4 tests covering:
  - Remote URL without explicit models returns null
  - Remote URL with explicit models returns only those models
  - Remote URL does not call `buildProvider`
  - Local URL still auto-discovers

## Validation

- All 31 tests in `discovery-shared.test.ts` pass
- Existing `isLocalOllamaBaseUrl` tests unchanged
- No changes to runtime behavior for local Ollama instances

## What was not tested

- Real network calls to cloud Ollama instances were not exercised
- The fix was validated via unit tests with mock providers only
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
